gpt55_review_summary: Laplace the 2nd found that a mixed empty-hash and hashed manifest state could return a stale empty-hash ref. The lookup now treats empty-hash requests as logical-identity resolution and raises ambiguity when more than one variant exists; re-review was clean.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_benchmark_handoffs.py tests/test_vllm_smoke.py -q -> 89 passed
poetry run python -m compileall -q src tests -> passed
poetry check -> passed with existing duplicated project/tool.poetry metadata warnings
changed-file Databricks credential pattern scan -> no matches
what_changed: Manifest lookups with an empty content_hash now resolve a unique same-logical-identity manifest entry, including entries written with a content hash.
Manifest lookups with explicit non-empty content_hash remain exact-match strict.
Mixed empty-hash/hashed or multiple hashed variants for the same logical identity now raise an ambiguity error instead of selecting a stale or arbitrary ref.
why: The Databricks g6.8xlarge hot-payload run reached workload execution but failed during prepared handoff generation because the generator wrote hashed manifest refs while benchmark requests still addressed chunks by logical document/chunk identity.
